epic_title: Conflict Coach v1 — Full-Stack AI Mediation Web App
planning_assumptions: The invitee's pre-join display name (typed by initiator at case creation as 'What's their name?') is not in the spec's cases/create mutation input. We add inviteeName: v.optional(v.string()) to the cases table and pass it through cases/create.
Solo mode isSolo=true pre-binds the same userId to both initiator and invitee slots; partyStates has two rows for the same userId with different roles. The party toggle filters by role, not userId.
The spec's COST_LIMITED status is not defined in the schema's case status union. It is added to cases.status in T2.
cases.tokenCount: v.optional(v.number()) is added to the cases table in T2 for AI cost tracking.
Convex Auth is the chosen auth provider (TQ1). The Clerk swap path is documented in README but not implemented.
Admin role promotion is a manual Convex Dashboard action in v1; no self-serve admin claim UI is built.
Typing indicators ('Jordan is typing...') reserve visual space per the design doc but Convex Presence logic is deferred to v1.1 per tech spec §8.3.
The decline-invite path sets the case to CLOSED_ABANDONED per design doc §4.3; added to state machine in T8.
The /cases/:id/invite post-create screen is a distinct route; invitee display name comes from inviteeName.
P2 user stories (US-16 email notifications, US-17 voluntary sharing, US-18 PDF export, US-19 inline rephrase) are explicitly out of scope.
ToS and privacy policy are static placeholder /terms and /privacy routes, not full tasks; linked from auth screens and case creation.

Cost tracking: tokenCount is accumulated per AI action call. Soft cap $2 ≈ 600k Sonnet tokens (hardcoded pricing constant); hard cap $10. Pricing constant is isolated in a single config file for easy update.

T17 budget enforcement logic and error-handling hardening is designed to run after T12-T15 as a cross-cutting enhancement. Token accumulation ACs are embedded in T12-T15 themselves; T17 adds the cap enforcement, degradation behavior, and Anthropic error handling.
dependency_graph_notes: Four strata: (1) Infrastructure [T1-T2], (2) Core modules [T3-T11]: auth, authorization, privacy filter, prompts, compression, state machine are parallel after T2+T4; templates backend (T11) unblocks case creation (T9); (3) Feature backends [T12-T17]: private coaching → synthesis → joint chat entry mutation (T14a) → joint chat coach (T14b) → draft coach; closure parallel after T14a; cost tracking (T17) is a cross-cutting hardening pass after T12-T15; (4) Frontend [T18-T32]: design tokens + shared components (T19a, T19b) → all views. T33 (accessibility) and T34 (E2E) both depend on T30+T32 (all feature UI complete) but NOT on each other — they can run in parallel. T35 (unit tests) depends only on T5+T6+T7+T8 and can run very early. T36 (CI) waits for T33+T34+T35.

tasks.title: Project scaffolding and cold-start setup
tasks.summary: Initialize the project with Vite + React 18 + TypeScript, Tailwind v3, shadcn/ui, React Router v6, Convex client, ESLint + Prettier. Create .env.example, .gitignore, and README with the exact cold-start sequence from tech spec §11.3. Verify `git clone && npm install && npx convex dev && npm run dev` produces a running empty React app.
acceptance_criteria: `npm install` completes without errors on a clean checkout
`npx convex dev` starts without errors (Convex project configured)
`npm run dev` serves a React app at localhost:5173 with no console errors
ESLint and Prettier configs present; `npm run lint` and `npm run format:check` pass on the empty project
`tsc --noEmit` passes with strict mode enabled
README contains verbatim the cold-start sequence from tech spec §11.3
.env.example includes all env vars from §11.2: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDE_MOCK, GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET, SITE_URL, MAGIC_LINK_EMAIL_FROM, VITE_CONVEX_URL, VITE_SITE_URL
stack_practices: Bootstrap with `npm create vite@latest -- --template react-ts`; do NOT use CRA.

Install Convex client: `npm install convex`; init project: `npx convex dev` (first run prompts project creation). The generated `convex/_generated/` directory must be gitignored — it is recreated on every `npx convex dev` run.
Tailwind v3 setup: `npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer && npx tailwindcss init -p`. Content glob must include `./src/**/*.{ts,tsx}` and `./convex/**/*.ts`.
shadcn/ui init: `npx shadcn-ui@latest init`. Choose 'New York' style, CSS variables color strategy. This writes `src/components/ui/` and modifies `tailwind.config.js` — commit both.
React Router v6: `npm install react-router-dom`. Wrap app in `<BrowserRouter>` in main.tsx. Never use React Router v5 patterns (`<Switch>`, `component=` prop).
Wire Convex in main.tsx: `<ConvexProvider client={convex}><App /></ConvexProvider>` — Convex Auth adds `<ConvexAuthProvider>` wrapping `<ConvexProvider>`.
tsconfig.json: enable `strict: true`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`. These are locked — do not soften.
ESLint: use `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` and `eslint-plugin-react-refresh`. Prettier: use `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` for class sorting.
Anti-pattern: do NOT call `npx convex deploy` during dev scaffolding — `npx convex dev` is the development command; deploy is for production.

tasks.title: Convex schema definition (all tables, all fields)
tasks.summary: Implement convex/schema.ts exactly as specified in tech spec §3.1 plus planning-assumption additions. All 10 tables: users, cases, partyStates, privateMessages, jointMessages, draftSessions, draftMessages, inviteTokens, templates, templateVersions, auditLog. Cases table includes: COST_LIMITED in status union, inviteeName, tokenCount. Document all 6 key invariants as comments.
acceptance_criteria: All 10 tables match tech spec §3.1 field names, types, and union literals exactly

cases.status union includes COST_LIMITED in addition to the 7 spec values: DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING, BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING, READY_FOR_JOINT, JOINT_ACTIVE, CLOSED_RESOLVED, CLOSED_UNRESOLVED, CLOSED_ABANDONED
cases table includes: inviteeName: v.optional(v.string()), tokenCount: v.optional(v.number())
partyStates table includes: closureProposalSummary: v.optional(v.string()) for the closure proposal flow
All indexes present: by_email, by_initiator, by_invitee, by_case, by_case_and_user, by_token, by_template, by_actor, by_draft_session, by_case (on jointMessages, privateMessages, draftMessages)
`npx convex dev` applies the schema without errors
All 6 key invariants from §3.2 documented as inline comments
`tsc --noEmit` passes
stack_practices: Use `defineSchema` and `defineTable` from `convex/server`; use `v` from `convex/values` for all field validators — never use raw TypeScript types here.
ID references use `v.id('tableName')` not `v.string()`. This gives compile-time type safety via `Id<'tableName'>` in generated types.
Union literals use `v.union(v.literal('A'), v.literal('B'))` — not `v.string()`. Convex validates at runtime; the TypeScript generated type becomes a string union.
Indexes: each `.index('name', ['field1', 'field2'])` call creates a compound index usable with `.withIndex('name', q => q.eq('field1', val))` in queries. Index names must be unique per table.
Anti-pattern: do NOT add `v.any()` fields except where explicitly needed (auditLog.metadata). Every `v.any()` sacrifices type safety end-to-end.
After editing schema.ts, `npx convex dev` must be running to push the schema; TypeScript types in `convex/_generated/dataModel.d.ts` regenerate automatically.
Convex does not support nullable without `v.optional()`. There is no `v.null()` in Convex v1 — use `v.optional(v.string())` to represent a nullable string.
The `schemaVersion: v.literal(1)` pattern on `cases` is intentional: it allows future data migrations to be detectable. Do not remove it.

tasks.title: Authentication — Convex Auth with magic link and Google OAuth
tasks.summary: Configure Convex Auth with email magic link and Google OAuth. On first login, upsert a users row (email, displayName from provider, role: 'USER'). Session persists across reloads until explicit logout or 30-day expiry. Wire ConvexAuthProvider and ConvexProvider into main.tsx.
acceptance_criteria: User registers and logs in via magic link: email submission sends a link; clicking the link establishes a session
User logs in via Google OAuth: OAuth flow completes and establishes a session
First login via either provider creates a users row with role='USER' and correct email
Subsequent logins upsert (do not duplicate) the users row
Session persists across browser reload (reactive query confirms identity)
Session expires after 30 days of inactivity
ctx.auth.getUserIdentity() returns the identity in any Convex function after login
stack_practices: Install: `npm install @convex-dev/auth @auth/core`. Convex Auth is a first-party library; do not use `next-auth` or Clerk here.
Auth config file is `convex/auth.config.ts` (not auth.ts). It exports a `convexAuth` default that lists providers: `Password` (for magic link) and `Google`.
Magic link provider: use `Resend` email provider from `@auth/core/providers/resend` or the Convex Auth `MagicLink` helper. Configure `MAGIC_LINK_EMAIL_FROM` env var in Convex dashboard.
Google OAuth: requires `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET` in Convex environment variables (not `.env` — set via `npx convex env set`).
Client side: import `useAuthActions` from `@convex-dev/auth/react` for `signIn`, `signOut`. Import `useConvexAuth` from `convex/react` for `isAuthenticated`, `isLoading`.

User upsert: create `convex/users.ts` with an internal mutation `upsertUser` called from the auth `onCreateOrUpdateUser` callback in auth.config.ts. Use `.index('by_email')` + `ctx.db.get()` pattern to detect existing rows.
Anti-pattern: do NOT store auth tokens in localStorage manually — Convex Auth manages the session cookie/token lifecycle.
Session check in Convex functions: `const identity = await ctx.auth.getUserIdentity()`. If null → throw ConvexError with code UNAUTHENTICATED. Do not use `ctx.auth.isLoggedIn()` — it doesn't exist.
ConvexAuthProvider must wrap ConvexProvider in main.tsx: `<ConvexAuthProvider client={convex}><App/></ConvexAuthProvider>`.

tasks.title: Authorization helper module and ConvexError codes
tasks.summary: Create convex/lib/auth.ts with requireAuth(ctx) helper, assertPartyToCase, assertAdmin. Define all 9 ConvexError codes from tech spec §7.4. Create convex/lib/pricing.ts with ANTHROPIC_PRICING constant (soft cap $2, hard cap $10) as an isolated config — one place to update when pricing changes.
acceptance_criteria: requireAuth(ctx) returns { identity, user } or throws ConvexError({ code: 'UNAUTHENTICATED', httpStatus: 401 })
All 9 error codes typed as constants: UNAUTHENTICATED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, CONFLICT, INVALID_INPUT, TOKEN_INVALID, RATE_LIMITED, AI_ERROR, INTERNAL
ConvexError shape: { code: string, message: string, httpStatus: number }
assertPartyToCase throws FORBIDDEN if caller is neither initiatorUserId nor inviteeUserId
assertAdmin throws FORBIDDEN if user.role !== 'ADMIN'
convex/lib/pricing.ts exports: SOFT_CAP_USD = 2.0, HARD_CAP_USD = 10.0, SONNET_INPUT_PRICE_PER_TOKEN, SONNET_OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_TOKEN, HAIKU_INPUT_PRICE_PER_TOKEN, HAIKU_OUTPUT_PRICE_PER_TOKEN
All subsequent Convex functions import error codes from convex/lib/auth.ts consistently
stack_practices: Import `ConvexError` from `convex/values` — this is Convex's built-in typed error class, NOT a custom class. Thrown ConvexErrors are surfaced to the client as structured data (not opaque 500s).
Error shape: `throw new ConvexError({ code: 'FORBIDDEN', message: '...', httpStatus: 403 })`. Client catches via the `ConvexError` type guard in `convex/react`.

Helper function signature: `async function requireAuth(ctx: QueryCtx | MutationCtx | ActionCtx): Promise<{ identity: UserIdentity; user: Doc<'users'> }>`. Import `QueryCtx`, `MutationCtx`, `ActionCtx` from `convex/_generated/server`.
`Doc<'users'>` is the generated Convex document type for the users table — always use generated types, never hand-craft them.

File location: `convex/lib/auth.ts` and `convex/lib/pricing.ts`. The `convex/lib/` subdirectory is for shared utilities that are NOT exported Convex functions (no `query`/`mutation`/`action` exports).
Anti-pattern: do NOT put authorization logic inline in each function — always call `requireAuth` and the assertion helpers. This prevents accidental omissions.

Pricing constants: token prices as of May 2026: Sonnet ~$3/$15 per 1M input/output, Haiku ~$0.25/$1.25. These are hardcoded; the isolation in pricing.ts means one-file update when Anthropic changes pricing.

tasks.title: Privacy response filter module
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/privacyFilter.ts: given a candidate AI response string and a set of other-party message strings, detect any ≥8 consecutive whitespace-token substring from the other-party messages appearing verbatim (case-insensitive) in the response. Returns { passed: boolean, matchedSubstring?: string }.
acceptance_criteria: filterResponse(response: string, otherPartyMessages: string[]): { passed: boolean, matchedSubstring?: string }
passed=false when any 8+ consecutive token window from other-party messages appears verbatim in response (case-insensitive)
passed=true when no such match exists
Handles empty otherPartyMessages → passed=true
Handles empty response → passed=true
Unit test: exact 10-word phrase from other party in response → passed=false
Unit test: response rephrases → passed=true
Unit test: 7-token substring (below threshold) → passed=true
Unit test: exact 8-token substring → passed=false (boundary case)
Performance: 10k-word message set processed without timeout in Vitest
stack_practices: This is a pure TypeScript module in `convex/lib/` — no Convex runtime imports, no DB calls, no SDK calls. It must be importable from both Convex actions and Vitest unit tests without side effects.

Tokenize by whitespace: `text.toLowerCase().split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean)`. Sliding window of size 8 over the tokenized other-party messages, reconstructed as joined strings, then check `response.toLowerCase().includes(window)`. O(n*m) is acceptable for the expected message sizes.
Vitest test file: `src/__tests__/privacyFilter.test.ts` (or `convex/lib/__tests__/privacyFilter.test.ts`). Import directly — no Convex test harness needed for pure functions.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use embedding similarity or any ML model here — the spec explicitly requires regex/substring matching. Complexity should be minimal.
Performance note: for a 10k-word message set, pre-compute all N-gram windows into a Set<string> for O(1) lookup per window rather than re-iterating per response check.
The function must be synchronous — no async. Convex actions can call it directly within the action body.

tasks.title: Prompt assembly module for all four AI roles
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/prompts.ts with assemblePrompt(opts) for PRIVATE_COACH, SYNTHESIS, COACH, DRAFT_COACH. Each returns { system: string, messages: Message[] }. All system prompts include explicit no-quote rules and crisis safety language. PRIVATE_COACH includes keyword-level crisis detection instructions.
acceptance_criteria: assemblePrompt({ role: 'PRIVATE_COACH' }) system prompt matches tech spec §6.3.1 verbatim plus crisis guardrail
assemblePrompt({ role: 'SYNTHESIS' }) produces a prompt requiring strict { forInitiator: string, forInvitee: string } JSON per §6.3.2
assemblePrompt({ role: 'COACH' }) incorporates templateVersion.globalGuidance + coachInstructions when provided
assemblePrompt({ role: 'DRAFT_COACH' }) incorporates draftCoachInstructions; explicitly excludes other party's synthesis
All four system prompts contain: 'NEVER quote, closely paraphrase, or otherwise surface the other party's raw words'
Function is pure TypeScript — no DB calls, no SDK calls; testable in Vitest
Unit test: SYNTHESIS system string contains verbatim critical instruction from §6.3.2
Unit test: DRAFT_COACH context assembly does not include other party's synthesis text even when provided in opts
stack_practices: Pure TypeScript module — no Convex imports, no `@anthropic-ai/sdk` imports. The function signature accepts plain data (strings, arrays) and returns `{ system: string; messages: Array<{ role: 'user' | 'assistant'; content: string }> }`. This matches the Anthropic SDK `MessageParam` shape.

Message type: define a local `type Message = { role: 'user' | 'assistant'; content: string }` — do NOT import from `@anthropic-ai/sdk` here, as that would create an SDK dependency in a pure utility module.
Template string approach: use tagged template literals or plain string concatenation. Do NOT use a templating library (Handlebars, Mustache, etc.) — unnecessary dependency.
Vitest tests: run in Node without any Convex infrastructure. `import { assemblePrompt } from '../convex/lib/prompts'` and assert string contents directly.
Anti-pattern: do NOT embed multi-paragraph prompt strings as escape-heavy one-liners. Use multi-line template literals for readability.

SYNTHESIS output format: the system prompt must instruct the model to respond with ONLY the JSON object `{ "forInitiator": "...", "forInvitee": "..." }` and nothing else. Include explicit instruction: 'Respond ONLY with valid JSON. No markdown, no preamble.'

tasks.title: Transcript compression module
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/transcriptCompression.ts: when token count exceeds budget (60k for user-facing, 10k for classification), take oldest 50% of messages, call Claude Haiku with compression prompt, replace with SUMMARY message. Cache by content hash.
acceptance_criteria: compressIfNeeded(messages, budgetTokens, anthropic): returns original array when under budget without any API call
When over budget: oldest 50% replaced by { role: 'system', content: 'SUMMARY: ...' } from Haiku
Compression prompt calls claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
Content hash caching: same message set compressed twice makes only one Haiku API call
Token counting uses word-count approximation (4 chars ≈ 1 token)
Unit test: array exceeding 60k budget is reduced; API not called for under-budget arrays
stack_practices: Function signature: `async function compressIfNeeded(messages: Message[], budgetTokens: number, anthropic: Anthropic): Promise<Message[]>`. Accepts the Anthropic client as a parameter (dependency injection) so unit tests can pass a mock.

Cache implementation: use a module-level `Map<string, string>` as the hash cache. For Convex actions, module-level state persists across calls within the same worker process — this is intentional and supported.

Content hash: use `JSON.stringify(messages)` fed into a simple non-cryptographic hash (e.g., FNV-1a or djb2 implemented in ~10 lines). Do NOT use Node's `crypto` module — Convex actions run in a V8 isolate without Node built-ins. Use `TextEncoder` + manual hash loop instead.
Haiku API call: `anthropic.messages.create({ model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', max_tokens: 600, messages: [...] })` — non-streaming, synchronous single call.
Anti-pattern: do NOT call `compressIfNeeded` with `budgetTokens=0` — guard against zero budget at the call site.
Vitest mock: `vi.fn()` or `vi.spyOn()` on the passed `anthropic` object's `messages.create` method. Verify call count to confirm caching works (second call with same messages = 1 total API call).

tasks.title: Case state machine module (all transitions including decline-invite)
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/stateMachine.ts with pure transition functions for all case states. Covered transitions including decline-invite and COST_LIMITED.
acceptance_criteria: Each transition function is pure: takes current case state + relevant party states, returns new status or throws ConvexError CONFLICT
DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING→CLOSED_ABANDONED: triggered by invitee declining; explicitly tested
BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING→READY_FOR_JOINT: requires both partyStates.privateCoachingCompletedAt set AND both synthesisTexts populated
READY_FOR_JOINT→JOINT_ACTIVE: any party can trigger; idempotent if already JOINT_ACTIVE
JOINT_ACTIVE→CLOSED_RESOLVED: requires other party's closureProposed=true
Sending a joint message when case is not JOINT_ACTIVE throws CONFLICT
All pure TypeScript, no DB calls — fully unit-testable
Unit test: all 9 valid transitions succeed from correct precondition state
Unit test: 8+ illegal transitions each throw CONFLICT
stack_practices: Pure TypeScript module — zero imports from Convex runtime or `@anthropic-ai/sdk`. All inputs are plain values (status strings, booleans, timestamps). Throws `ConvexError` from `convex/values` (this IS an allowed import since `convex/values` is a pure type/value package).

Export one named function per transition, e.g. `transitionToJointActive`, `transitionToBothPrivateCoaching`, etc. Do NOT export a single god-function `transition(from, to, state)` — individual functions are easier to test and audit.

Status type: `type CaseStatus = 'DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING' | 'BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING' | ...` derived from the schema's union. Define it once here and import it into Convex functions — do NOT redefine it.
Vitest: each transition test is a synchronous call with fabricated state objects. No Convex test harness needed. Use `expect(() => fn(...)).toThrow(ConvexError)` pattern.

Anti-pattern: do NOT read from `ctx.db` inside state machine functions — all DB reads happen in the calling mutation/action, which then passes the values to the state machine. This is the explicit contract for testability.
Idempotency: `READY_FOR_JOINT→JOINT_ACTIVE` should return the current status without throwing if already `JOINT_ACTIVE`. Pattern: `if (currentStatus === 'JOINT_ACTIVE') return currentStatus;`

tasks.title: Case CRUD backend (create, get, list, partyStates, updateMyForm)
tasks.summary: Implement Convex queries and mutations for case management: cases/create, cases/get, cases/list, cases/partyStates, cases/updateMyForm. All enforce auth + party-membership rules.
acceptance_criteria: cases/create: creates cases row, partyStates row for initiator, inviteTokens row; returns caseId + invite URL
cases/create with isSolo=true: pre-binds invitee, sets BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING
cases/list: returns only caller's cases
cases/get: returns NOT_FOUND if caller not a party
cases/partyStates: returns self (full) and other (phase-only, no raw text)
cases/updateMyForm: updates caller's partyStates fields
Token stored as plaintext per §12 note
stack_practices: File structure: `convex/cases.ts` exporting `create`, `get`, `list`, `partyStates`, `updateMyForm` using the `query`/`mutation` wrappers from `convex/_generated/server`.
Convex function export pattern: `export const create = mutation({ args: { ... }, handler: async (ctx, args) => { ... } })`. Args are validated by Convex at runtime using the `v` validators.

Crypto-random token in Convex mutations: use `crypto.randomUUID().replace(/-/g, '') + crypto.randomUUID().replace(/-/g, '')` sliced to 32 chars. `crypto` is available globally in Convex's V8 environment.

Index queries: `await ctx.db.query('inviteTokens').withIndex('by_token', q => q.eq('token', token)).unique()` — use `.unique()` when expecting at most one result; it throws if multiple rows match (schema bug surfaced early).

cases/list: use `.withIndex('by_initiator', q => q.eq('initiatorUserId', user._id))` + a second query for `by_invitee`, then merge and deduplicate. Or query all cases where either index matches via parallel reads.

cases/partyStates privacy: NEVER return synthesisText or privateMessages content for the 'other' party object. Return only `{ role, formCompletedAt: boolean, privateCoachingCompletedAt: boolean }` — strip all sensitive fields explicitly.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use `ctx.db.query('cases').collect()` without an index — full table scans grow linearly with data. Always use `.withIndex()`.
Site URL: read `process.env.SITE_URL` in Convex actions/mutations. For dev: set via `npx convex env set SITE_URL http://localhost:5173`.

tasks.title: Invite flow backend (redeem mutation, preview query, token validation)
tasks.summary: Implement invites/redeem mutation and invites/preview query.
acceptance_criteria: invites/redeem: validates token, atomically binds invitee, creates partyStates, marks token CONSUMED, advances case to BOTH_PRIVATE_COACHING
invites/redeem: throws CONFLICT if caller is already the initiator
invites/redeem on CONSUMED token: throws TOKEN_INVALID
invites/preview: returns category, initiatorDisplayName, mainTopic, inviteeName with NO private content
invites/preview on non-existent token: returns null
stack_practices: File: `convex/invites.ts`. Export `redeem` (mutation) and `preview` (query).
Atomicity: Convex mutations are fully transactional — all `ctx.db.patch`, `ctx.db.insert` calls within one mutation either all succeed or all fail. No need for manual rollback logic.
`ctx.db.patch(id, fields)` for partial updates; `ctx.db.replace(id, fullDoc)` for full replacement. Use `patch` for token status update to avoid accidentally overwriting unrelated fields.
invites/preview is a Convex `query` (not mutation) — it only reads. Even though it uses a token (public identifier), it returns only non-sensitive fields. No auth required for preview.
Anti-pattern: do NOT check token validity in invites/preview — it's intentionally public so the logged-out invite landing page can show a preview before the user authenticates.
Self-invite prevention: compare `cases.initiatorUserId` with `user._id` in the redeem handler. Throw `ConvexError({ code: 'CONFLICT', ... })` if they match (non-solo mode).

tasks.title: Templates backend and seed data
tasks.summary: Implement template management queries and mutations. Create convex/seed.ts with one admin user and three default templates.
acceptance_criteria: templates/list: returns non-archived templates
admin/templates/create: creates template + initial version; writes to auditLog
admin/templates/publishNewVersion: inserts new immutable row; updates currentVersionId
admin/templates/archive: sets archivedAt; existing cases unaffected
All admin mutations throw FORBIDDEN for non-ADMIN users
convex/seed.ts: creates admin user and three templates; throws INTERNAL if outside dev
stack_practices: File structure: `convex/templates.ts` for user-facing queries; `convex/admin/templates.ts` for admin mutations. Convex supports subdirectory function files — path becomes the function name prefix (`admin/templates/create`).
Immutability of templateVersions: enforced by absence of an update mutation. There is no `ctx.db.patch` call for templateVersions anywhere in the codebase. Document this with a comment in the file.

Seed file: `convex/seed.ts` should export a mutation (not action), callable via `npx convex run seed`. Guard with `if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production') throw new ConvexError(...)` or check `ctx.auth` is an admin.
AuditLog writes: always use a helper `writeAuditLog(ctx, { actorUserId, action, targetType, targetId, metadata })` defined in `convex/lib/auditLog.ts` to prevent forgetting fields.

Pinned case count query: use `ctx.db.query('cases').withIndex('by_template_version', ...)` — but since there's no such index, query all cases and filter client-side, OR add an index `by_template_version_id: ['templateVersionId']` to cases in T2 (recommended). Flag this dependency.
Anti-pattern: do NOT version templates by mutating the existing row — always insert a new `templateVersions` row. The `templateVersions` table name signals immutability.

tasks.title: Private coaching backend (send message, AI response, mark complete, crisis detection, token tracking)
tasks.summary: Implement private coaching queries, mutations, and AI action.
acceptance_criteria: privateCoaching/myMessages: returns only caller's messages; FORBIDDEN if not party
privateCoaching/sendUserMessage: inserts user message, checks crisis keywords, schedules generateAIResponse
generateAIResponse: assembles PRIVATE_COACH prompt, applies compression, streams into privateMessages row
generateAIResponse: increments cases.tokenCount after completion
generateAIResponse: checks HARD_CAP before calling Claude
privateCoaching/markComplete: idempotent; schedules synthesis if both complete
Security test: User B querying User A's messages returns FORBIDDEN
stack_practices: File: `convex/privateCoaching.ts`. Export `myMessages` (query), `sendUserMessage` (mutation), `generateAIResponse` (action), `markComplete` (mutation).

Streaming pattern: (1) mutation inserts STREAMING row with empty content and returns the row ID; (2) action is scheduled via `ctx.scheduler.runAfter(0, internal.privateCoaching.generateAIResponse, args)`; (3) action calls `anthropic.messages.stream(...)` and in the `onText` callback accumulates tokens, flushing to DB via internal mutation every ~50ms (use `Date.now()` delta check); (4) on stream end, final patch sets status=COMPLETE.

Internal vs public functions: `generateAIResponse` must be an `internalAction` (from `convex/_generated/server`) — not callable from the client. Only `sendUserMessage` (mutation) triggers it via `ctx.scheduler`. This is the canonical Convex security pattern for AI actions.
Streaming flush mutation: define `_updateStreamingMessage` as an `internalMutation` in the same file. Actions cannot call ctx.db directly — they must go through internal mutations.

Token accumulation: after stream completes, call an internal mutation `_incrementTokenCount(ctx, { caseId, tokens: usage.input_tokens + usage.output_tokens })`. Do NOT try to do this inside the action directly.
Anti-pattern: do NOT call `generateAIResponse` directly from the client — it must be an `internalAction`. Exposing AI actions to the public API creates a SSRF/abuse vector.
CLAUDE_MOCK=true: in the action body, check `process.env.CLAUDE_MOCK === 'true'` and return a canned response with a simulated 200ms delay instead of calling Anthropic. This is the E2E test stub.

tasks.title: AI synthesis backend (generate action, privacy filter, state transition)
tasks.summary: Implement synthesis/generate action: reads both parties' private messages, assembles SYNTHESIS prompt, calls Claude Sonnet (non-streaming), validates JSON output, runs privacy filter, writes synthesisText, advances case to READY_FOR_JOINT.
acceptance_criteria: synthesis/generate reads privateMessages for BOTH parties via by_case index (server-side only)
Calls claude-sonnet-4-5; validates { forInitiator, forInvitee } JSON
Runs filterResponse on each half; retries up to 2x on privacy filter failure
Privacy filter failure fallback: writes SYNTHESIS_FILTER_FAILED to auditLog; uses generic text
On success: writes synthesisText + synthesisGeneratedAt to both partyStates; sets case READY_FOR_JOINT
Increments tokenCount
Idempotent: if synthesisText already populated, returns without re-generating
stack_practices: File: `convex/synthesis.ts`. Export `generate` as an `internalAction` only — scheduled by `privateCoaching/markComplete`, never called directly by the client.

Non-streaming call: `await anthropic.messages.create({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-5', max_tokens: 2048, messages, system })` returns the full response. Parse with `JSON.parse(response.content[0].text)` and validate shape with a simple type guard.

JSON validation: `function isValidSynthesisOutput(v: unknown): v is { forInitiator: string; forInvitee: string }` — check `typeof v === 'object'`, field presence, and string type. Do NOT use Zod inside Convex functions (adds bundle size with little benefit at this scale).
Atomic write: after successful synthesis, update BOTH partyStates rows and the case status in a single internal mutation. Convex mutations are transactions — either all writes succeed or none do.

Retry loop: implement as a for loop `for (let attempt = 0; attempt < 3; attempt++)`. On 3rd failure, write auditLog entry and use fallback string. Never use recursion for retry in Convex actions (stack depth concerns).
Anti-pattern: do NOT call the Anthropic API inside a Convex mutation — mutations cannot make HTTP calls. Synthesis generation MUST happen in an action.

tasks.title: Joint chat entry mutation and Coach facilitation backend
tasks.summary: Implement jointChat/enter, jointChat/messages, jointChat/mySynthesis, jointChat/sendUserMessage, and generateCoachResponse action with Haiku classification gate.
acceptance_criteria: jointChat/enter: READY_FOR_JOINT→JOINT_ACTIVE, idempotent, schedules OPENING Coach message
jointChat/messages: FORBIDDEN if not party; CONFLICT if case in private-only state
jointChat/sendUserMessage: detects @-mention internally; schedules generateCoachResponse
generateCoachResponse OPENING: Sonnet opening message grounded in case
generateCoachResponse USER_MESSAGE: Haiku classifies; Coach only for non-NORMAL_EXCHANGE
generateCoachResponse MENTION: always generates Sonnet response
T5 filter on Coach Sonnet response; fallback on failure
Increments tokenCount; enforces HARD_CAP
stack_practices: File: `convex/jointChat.ts`. Queries: `messages`, `mySynthesis`. Mutations: `enter`, `sendUserMessage`, `proposeClosure`, `confirmClosure`, `rejectClosure`, `unilateralClose`. Internal actions: `generateCoachResponse`.

Haiku classification: single non-streaming call with `max_tokens: 50` and a classification system prompt. Parse response text for one of `INFLAMMATORY | PROGRESS | QUESTION_TO_COACH | NORMAL_EXCHANGE`. If response doesn't match any label (malformed), treat as `NORMAL_EXCHANGE` (safe default).

TIMER trigger: check count of user messages since the last COACH jointMessage BEFORE scheduling — do this synchronously in `sendUserMessage` mutation by querying `jointMessages` with `by_case` index and filtering client-side. Convex queries within mutations are consistent reads.
Streaming for Coach messages: same pattern as T12. Insert STREAMING jointMessage row (with `authorType: 'COACH'`, `authorUserId: null`) → stream tokens → patch to COMPLETE.

isIntervention flag: set `isIntervention: true` on the jointMessage row when triggerType was `USER_MESSAGE` AND Haiku classified the message as `INFLAMMATORY`. Do NOT set it for MENTION or TIMER triggers.

Anti-pattern: do NOT hardcode @-mention as a client-side feature that passes `triggerType` from the client. The spec explicitly says @-mention detection happens inside the mutation body (`content.toLowerCase().includes('@coach')`). Clients cannot specify triggerType.

CLAUDE_MOCK guard: same `process.env.CLAUDE_MOCK` check as T12. For the Haiku classification stub, return `NORMAL_EXCHANGE` by default (so tests don't trigger Coach responses unless explicitly testing intervention).

tasks.title: Draft Coach backend (session lifecycle, AI conversation, readiness detection, send gate)
tasks.summary: Implement draftCoach/session, startSession, sendMessage, generateResponse, sendFinalDraft, discardSession.
acceptance_criteria: draftCoach/startSession: creates draftSessions row; throws CONFLICT if case not JOINT_ACTIVE
draftCoach/sendMessage: inserts draftMessages; schedules generateResponse
generateResponse: context = drafting user's joint chat history + own synthesisText ONLY
Readiness detection: produces { 'draft': '...' } JSON; writes to draftSessions.finalDraft
Non-readiness turns: stream conversational response into draftMessages
draftCoach/sendFinalDraft: reads finalDraft; calls jointChat/sendUserMessage internally
draftCoach/discardSession: sets status=DISCARDED
Draft Coach conversation invisible to other party
stack_practices: File: `convex/draftCoach.ts`. Query: `session` (returns session + messages). Mutations: `startSession`, `sendMessage`, `sendFinalDraft`, `discardSession`. Internal action: `generateResponse`.

draftCoach/sendFinalDraft calling jointChat/sendUserMessage: use `ctx.runMutation(internal.jointChat.sendUserMessage, { caseId, content: session.finalDraft })`. This is an internal cross-function call — safe within the same Convex deployment.

Readiness detection: the action checks the incoming user message text against the readiness phrase list BEFORE calling Claude for a full response. If readiness detected, call Claude once with a 'produce the draft JSON' instruction (max_tokens ~300). If not, call Claude for a conversational response (max_tokens ~400, streaming).
Draft JSON validation: parse `JSON.parse(response)` and check for `{ draft: string }` shape. If parsing fails, treat as a non-readiness conversational response and stream it as normal.

Privacy enforcement: the action must NOT read `partyStates.synthesisText` for the other party. Read ONLY the caller's partyStates row by `by_case_and_user` index. Explicitly document this constraint in a code comment.

Anti-pattern: do NOT use `ctx.db.query('jointMessages').collect()` to build context — use `.withIndex('by_case')` with a `.order('asc')` and limit to the most recent N messages (e.g., 50) to keep context manageable before compression kicks in.

tasks.title: Case closure backend (propose, confirm, reject, unilateral close, abandoned cron)
tasks.summary: Implement closure mutations and convex/crons.ts daily job for CLOSED_ABANDONED detection.
acceptance_criteria: jointChat/proposeClosure: sets closureProposed; writes to auditLog
jointChat/confirmClosure: atomically closes case as CLOSED_RESOLVED; writes to auditLog
jointChat/rejectClosure: clears proposal; writes to auditLog
jointChat/unilateralClose: sets CLOSED_UNRESOLVED; writes to auditLog
Closed cases reject further mutations with CONFLICT
Cron: daily scan for JOINT_ACTIVE cases with 30d inactivity → CLOSED_ABANDONED
stack_practices: Cron setup: create `convex/crons.ts` exporting a `cronJobs` object using `cronJobs()` from `convex/server`. Register: `crons.daily('abandon-stale-cases', { hourUTC: 2, minuteUTC: 0 }, internal.cases.abandonStaleCases)`. This is the ONLY supported cron API in Convex — do NOT use `setInterval` or external cron services.

Cron action body: query `jointMessages` by case, grouped by caseId, find max `createdAt` per case. Cases with max `createdAt` < `Date.now() - 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000` get patched to `CLOSED_ABANDONED`. Process in batches if needed (Convex actions have a 10-minute timeout).

Closure confirmation atomicity: `jointChat/confirmClosure` must read both partyStates rows, verify `closureProposed=true` on the other party, then patch the case status + closedAt + closureSummary in a single mutation. If the other party's row is NOT found as proposed, throw CONFLICT.

System message on rejection: optionally insert a jointMessage with `authorType: 'COACH'` and content 'The closure proposal was rejected. Continue your conversation.' — this is a regular DB insert, not an AI call.
Anti-pattern: do NOT implement a polling fallback for the cron. If the cron misses a run (Convex downtime), it will run at the next scheduled time. 30-day abandonment is not time-critical.

tasks.title: AI cost tracking hardening: budget enforcement, soft-cap degradation, Anthropic error handling
tasks.summary: Cross-cutting hardening pass: soft-cap degradation, hard-cap enforcement, Anthropic error handling across all AI actions.
acceptance_criteria: Soft cap: generateCoachResponse posts boilerplate instead of Sonnet at $2 threshold
Hard cap: all four AI actions return early at $10; post system message; set COST_LIMITED
429: retry once after 2000ms; second 429 → RATE_LIMITED error
Network timeout >30s: message status=ERROR; throw AI_ERROR
401/403: message status=ERROR; write API_KEY_ERROR to auditLog; throw AI_ERROR
Content filter: message status=ERROR with rephrase suggestion
Integration tests: seed cases at cap thresholds; verify enforcement
stack_practices: Cap check: at the top of each AI action, compute `estimatedCostUsd = (case.tokenCount ?? 0) * avgPricePerToken` using values from `convex/lib/pricing.ts`. Check against `SOFT_CAP_USD` and `HARD_CAP_USD` before ANY Anthropic call.

COST_LIMITED state transition: when hard cap is hit, call an internal mutation that sets `cases.status = 'COST_LIMITED'` and inserts a system jointMessage. This internal mutation can be shared across all four AI actions.

Anthropic SDK error handling: wrap all `anthropic.messages.create()` / `stream()` calls in try/catch. The SDK throws `APIError` with a `.status` property. Check `error.status === 429` for rate limit, `401/403` for auth errors.
Retry implementation: `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 2000))` then retry once. Do NOT use exponential backoff for the first retry — the spec says a fixed 2s delay.
Timeout: wrap the Anthropic call with `Promise.race([apiCall, new Promise((_, reject) => setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('timeout')), 30000))])`.

Anti-pattern: do NOT let AI errors bubble up as unhandled Convex action failures — unhandled action failures show as generic 500 errors in the client. Always catch, set message status=ERROR, and throw a typed ConvexError.
For convex-test integration tests: use `convex-test` package to run against the real Convex runtime in-process with seeded data. Do NOT mock `ctx.db` — test against real document reads/writes.

tasks.title: Design tokens and global styles
tasks.summary: Implement all CSS custom properties from design doc §2.1, Tailwind config mapping, Inter + JetBrains Mono fonts, dark mode via prefers-color-scheme, shadow levels, and motion tokens.
acceptance_criteria: All 16 color tokens defined as CSS custom properties in src/index.css
Dark mode via @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)
Tailwind config maps each token to a semantic utility class
Inter variable font loaded (400/500/600); JetBrains Mono loaded
Four shadow utilities matching design doc values
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce): all transitions 0ms, animations paused
stack_practices: CSS custom properties go in `src/index.css` under `:root { --canvas: ...; }` and `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { :root { --canvas: ...; } }`. Do NOT put tokens in JavaScript.
Tailwind config extension: in `tailwind.config.js`, under `theme.extend.colors`, map tokens: `canvas: 'var(--canvas)'`, etc. This enables `bg-canvas` class in JSX.

Font loading: use `@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400&display=swap')` in `src/index.css`. Alternatively, self-host via `@fontsource/inter` and `@fontsource/jetbrains-mono` npm packages (better performance, no CORS).

shadcn/ui uses CSS variables internally — the variable names in `src/index.css` must match the shadcn convention (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, etc.) to keep shadcn components styled correctly. Map your semantic tokens TO the shadcn variable names.
Tailwind v3 dark mode: set `darkMode: 'media'` in tailwind.config.js (NOT `'class'`). This activates dark mode based on `prefers-color-scheme: dark` media query, matching the CSS approach.

Anti-pattern: do NOT define color values in both `tailwind.config.js` and `src/index.css` separately — one source of truth. CSS custom properties are the source; Tailwind config references them via `var(--token-name)`.

tasks.title: Core chat UI components (ChatWindow, MessageBubble, StreamingIndicator, MessageInput)
tasks.summary: Build foundational chat primitives used by all three chat UIs.
acceptance_criteria: ChatWindow auto-scrolls to latest unless user has scrolled up >100px
MessageBubble USER: right-aligned, bg-surface; COACH/AI: left-aligned, accent-subtle, Sparkles icon
MessageBubble STREAMING: content + blinking cursor; no copy button
MessageBubble COMPLETE: copy button + timestamp on hover
MessageBubble ERROR: warning-tint + Retry button
StreamingIndicator: 500ms blink; aria-hidden
MessageInput: Enter sends; Shift+Enter newline; disabled while STREAMING
No accessibility defects: textarea has visible label or aria-label
stack_practices: Component files: `src/components/chat/ChatWindow.tsx`, `MessageBubble.tsx`, `MessageInput.tsx`, `StreamingIndicator.tsx`. Use named exports (not default exports) for all components.

Auto-scroll: use `useRef<HTMLDivElement>` on the scroll container and a `useEffect` that calls `ref.current.scrollTo({ top: ref.current.scrollHeight, behavior: 'smooth' })` when messages change, conditional on scroll position check (`ref.current.scrollTop + ref.current.clientHeight >= ref.current.scrollHeight - 100`).
shadcn/ui primitives to use: `Button` from `@/components/ui/button`; Lucide icons (`Sparkles`, `Copy`, `AlertTriangle`) from `lucide-react`. Do NOT install a separate icon library.
Copy to clipboard: `navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)` — the Web Clipboard API. Show a brief checkmark state (100ms delay via `useState`) on success.

StreamingIndicator animation: use `@keyframes blink { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1 } 50% { opacity: 0 } }` in CSS, applied via a Tailwind `animate-[blink_500ms_step-end_infinite]` custom animation or defined in `tailwind.config.js` under `theme.extend.animation`. Must be wrapped in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` — outside that query, no animation.
MessageInput keyboard: `onKeyDown` handler checks `e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey` → call `onSend`, `e.preventDefault()`. If `e.shiftKey`, do nothing (browser default inserts newline in textarea).
Anti-pattern: do NOT use `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` for message content rendering. Use a markdown renderer (`react-markdown` with `rehype-sanitize` plugin) to prevent XSS from AI-generated content.

tasks.title: Secondary UI components (PrivacyBanner, PartyAvatar, CoachIntervention, PhaseHeader, StatusPill, CrisisCard, skeletons)
tasks.summary: Build remaining shared components.
acceptance_criteria: PrivacyBanner: lock icon + Dialog explaining privacy; screen reader text
PartyAvatar: role-colored circles; shows first initial
CoachIntervention: coach-accent border, ⟡ glyph, accent-subtle background
PhaseHeader: current phase label + Back to Dashboard link
StatusPill: ●○◐◼ with correct color mapping
CrisisCard: appears on crisis keywords; dismissable; links to crisis resources
Dashboard skeleton: 3 case row skeletons
Case detail skeleton: phase-appropriate layout
stack_practices: shadcn/ui Dialog: `import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription } from '@/components/ui/dialog'`. The Dialog component handles focus trapping and Esc-close natively — do NOT reimplement these behaviors.
shadcn/ui Sheet: use for the 'My Guidance' panel and mobile Draft Coach. `import { Sheet, SheetContent, SheetHeader, SheetTitle } from '@/components/ui/sheet'`.

Skeleton components: use shadcn/ui `Skeleton` primitive (`import { Skeleton } from '@/components/ui/skeleton'`). Apply a delay before showing: `const [showSkeleton, setShowSkeleton] = useState(false); useEffect(() => { const t = setTimeout(() => setShowSkeleton(true), 300); return () => clearTimeout(t); }, [])`. This prevents skeleton flash for fast loads.

PartyAvatar: render as `
{initial}
`. Use inline style for dynamic colors if the three colors are not in the Tailwind safelist.

CrisisCard: should be a standalone component that accepts `isVisible` and `onDismiss` props. Store dismissed state in component-local `useState`. Re-appear logic (new crisis message) is handled by the parent resetting a key prop.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use raw `<dialog>` HTML elements. Use shadcn/ui Dialog which is built on Radix UI — it handles accessibility (ARIA roles, focus management, scroll locking) automatically.
Screen reader text: use the `sr-only` Tailwind class for text that should only be read by screen readers but not shown visually: `Private conversation. Only you and the AI coach see this.`.

tasks.title: Landing page and login/register page
tasks.summary: Implement / (hero, explainer, footer) and /login (magic link, Google OAuth, states). Wire Convex Auth signIn methods.
acceptance_criteria: / displays tagline, 3-step explainer, privacy section, footer with links
/ has 'Start a case' CTA routing to /login (logged out) or /dashboard (logged in)
/login: centered ~400px card; email + magic link button; Google OAuth button
Submitting email triggers magic link; form replaced by confirmation state
Error state inline (not a toast)
Fine print links to /terms and /privacy
After auth: redirect to /dashboard
stack_practices: Magic link submit: `const { signIn } = useAuthActions(); await signIn('resend', { email })`. This is the Convex Auth hook — do NOT use fetch/axios to call a custom endpoint.
Google OAuth: `await signIn('google')`. This opens the OAuth popup/redirect. No manual OAuth flow needed.
Auth redirect: use React Router's `useNavigate` hook. After successful sign-in, navigate to `/dashboard`. If `useConvexAuth().isAuthenticated` is true when loading `/login`, immediately redirect.
Protected route pattern: create a `<RequireAuth>` wrapper component that reads `useConvexAuth().isAuthenticated`. If false and loading is done, redirect to `/login?from=${location.pathname}`.

Error display: catch errors from `signIn` calls in try/catch and store in `useState`. Render inline below the triggering input: `{error &&

{error}

}`.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use `<form action=...>` or `<form method='POST'>` — this is a SPA. Handle form submission via `onSubmit={e => { e.preventDefault(); ... }}` and React state.
The '/' route redirect: use `useConvexAuth` to check authentication and React Router's `<Navigate>` component — do NOT use `window.location.href`.

tasks.title: Invite acceptance page (/invite/:token)
tasks.summary: Implement invite acceptance per design doc §4.3. Handles logged-out, logged-in unredeemed, consumed, and non-existent token states.
acceptance_criteria: Logged-out: explanation card + 'Sign in to continue'; token preserved through auth
Logged-in unredeemed: shows initiator name, mainTopic (labeled), category; no private content
Accept calls invites/redeem; routes to /cases/:id/private
Decline → CLOSED_ABANDONED → redirect with toast
CONSUMED token: 'This invite has already been used.'
Non-existent token: NOT_FOUND error message
stack_practices: Route param: `const { token } = useParams<{ token: string }>()` in React Router v6. The token is a URL segment, NOT a query param.

Token persistence through auth: store the token in `sessionStorage` (not localStorage) before redirecting to /login: `sessionStorage.setItem('pendingInviteToken', token)`. After auth, check sessionStorage and redirect back to `/invite/${token}`.
Preview query: `const preview = useQuery(api.invites.preview, { token: token! })`. `useQuery` returns `undefined` while loading, `null` if not found, or the data object. Handle all three states.
useMutation hook: `const redeem = useMutation(api.invites.redeem)`. Call `await redeem({ token })` in the Accept handler. Catch ConvexError by type: `import { ConvexError } from 'convex/values'`.
Toast: use shadcn/ui `toast` from `@/components/ui/use-toast`. Do NOT use a separate toast library.
Anti-pattern: do NOT call `invites/redeem` on page load — only call it when the user explicitly clicks Accept. The preview query is safe to call automatically; the mutation is not.

tasks.title: Dashboard page (/dashboard) with unilateral-close notification indicator
tasks.summary: Implement /dashboard: Active/Closed sections, case rows with status glyphs, Nudge button, empty state, [+ New Case] button, and notification indicator for unilateral closes.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches cases/list; renders skeleton while loading
Active/Closed sections with correct status glyph mapping
Unilateral close indicator: ◼ with red dot + 'Jordan closed this case.' for CLOSED_UNRESOLVED non-actor
Nudge button on DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING cases >7 days old without invitee
Empty state with link to /cases/new
Case rows keyboard-navigable; Enter activates
stack_practices: Data fetching: `const cases = useQuery(api.cases.list)`. This is a reactive query — the dashboard automatically updates when any case changes state (e.g., other party marks PC complete).
No additional polling needed: Convex reactive queries replace `setInterval` + fetch patterns. The UI will update within ~200ms of any server-side state change.

Closed section default collapsed: use `useState<boolean>(false)` for expanded state. Auto-expand when a case transitions to CLOSED_*: compare prev `cases` (from a `useRef`) with current in a `useEffect`.

React Router navigation: case row click → `navigate('/cases/${caseId}')`. Make the entire row keyboard-clickable: add `role='button' tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={e => e.key === 'Enter' && navigate(...)} onClick={() => navigate(...)}` to the row div.
Counterparty name logic: `case.inviteeUserId ? invitee.displayName : case.inviteeName ?? 'Invited Party'`. The inviteeName field handles the pre-join state.
Anti-pattern: do NOT derive status glyph logic in JSX directly — extract a `getStatusGlyph(caseStatus, isMyTurn): { glyph: string; color: string }` helper function and unit-test it separately.

tasks.title: Case creation form and post-create invite sharing screen
tasks.summary: Implement /cases/new (5-step progressive form) and /cases/:id/invite (share screen).
acceptance_criteria: Five steps with Back/Next navigation; Submit on step 5
Step 1: category radio cards for all 5 categories
Step 2: mainTopic with 140-char counter, labeled 'visible to other person'
Steps 3+4: lock icon + tooltip on private fields
Step 5: other party name; Advanced disclosure with solo mode
Crisis language prominently displayed
Submit calls cases/create; routes to /cases/:id/invite
/cases/:id/invite: copyable invite URL; email mailto pre-fill; 'Start coaching now' link
stack_practices: Multi-step form state: use `useState` for the current step index and a single state object for all form data. Do NOT use separate `useState` per field — one `FormData` object with `setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, field: value }))` pattern.

Character counter: `{mainTopic.length}/140` in JSX. Apply warning styles (`text-danger`) when `mainTopic.length > 140`. Do NOT use `maxLength` HTML attribute — allow over-limit with visual warning per spec.
Radio cards for category: use Tailwind to style `<input type='radio'>` as cards. OR use shadcn/ui `RadioGroup` + `RadioGroupItem`. Do NOT use a `<select>` dropdown.
shadcn/ui Tooltip: `import { Tooltip, TooltipContent, TooltipProvider, TooltipTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tooltip'`. Wrap the page in `<TooltipProvider>` once at the route level.
shadcn/ui Collapsible for 'Advanced': `import { Collapsible, CollapsibleContent, CollapsibleTrigger } from '@/components/ui/collapsible'`.
Clipboard API for invite URL: same `navigator.clipboard.writeText(inviteUrl)` pattern as MessageBubble copy.
mailto link: `href={\`mailto:?subject=...&body=...\`}` where body is `encodeURIComponent(templateText)`. Assemble from design doc §4.6 template text.

Anti-pattern: do NOT fetch `templates/list` on every step render — fetch once on component mount and memoize with `useRef` or component state. Template list is static for the duration of the form session.

tasks.title: Case detail router and phase-appropriate view scaffold
tasks.summary: Implement CaseDetail.tsx at /cases/:caseId routing to phase-appropriate sub-routes by case.status. Guard against phase-skipping.
acceptance_criteria: GET /cases/:caseId redirects to correct sub-route by case.status
Phase-skip redirects with toasts
PhaseHeader on all sub-routes
Loading: skeleton while cases/get in flight
caseId not found / not party: friendly error page
stack_practices: React Router v6 nested routes: `CaseDetail.tsx` is a layout route that renders `<Outlet />`. Sub-routes (`/private`, `/joint`, `/closed`) are children in the route definition.

Phase routing logic: in `CaseDetail.tsx`, after `cases/get` resolves, use a `useEffect` (or render-time check) to call `navigate` to the appropriate sub-route. Use `replace: true` to avoid polluting browser history with redirect entries.
useQuery loading state: Convex `useQuery` returns `undefined` while loading. Pattern: `if (caseData === undefined) return <CaseDetailSkeleton />`. `null` means not found.
Error boundary: wrap CaseDetail in a React error boundary component that catches Convex errors (ConvexError thrown in query handlers) and renders a friendly error page.
PhaseHeader as a shared layout element: render it in CaseDetail layout, above `<Outlet />`, so all sub-routes inherit it without re-implementing it.
Anti-pattern: do NOT re-fetch `cases/get` in each sub-route component — use React Router's `useOutletContext` to pass the already-fetched case data from CaseDetail to child routes.

tasks.title: Private coaching view (/cases/:id/private) with crisis card integration
tasks.summary: Implement PrivateCoachingView.tsx: full-height chat, PrivacyBanner, mark-complete footer CTA + confirmation modal, CrisisCard integration.
acceptance_criteria: Loads privateCoaching/myMessages reactively
PrivacyBanner always visible
Streaming/Complete/Error message states
Send disabled while STREAMING
Mark-complete footer CTA + confirmation modal
Routes to /ready if case advances to READY_FOR_JOINT
CrisisCard appears on crisis keywords; dismissable; re-appears on new crisis message
stack_practices: Reactive streaming: `const messages = useQuery(api.privateCoaching.myMessages, { caseId })`. As the AI action patches the streaming row, `useQuery` auto-delivers the update — no manual polling, no WebSocket code.
isStreaming detection: `const isStreaming = messages?.some(m => m.status === 'STREAMING') ?? false`. Pass to `<MessageInput isStreaming={isStreaming} />`.

Route transition on case advance: `const caseData = useOutletContext<{ case: Case }>()`. In a `useEffect`, watch `caseData.case.status` — when it becomes `READY_FOR_JOINT`, call `navigate('../ready', { replace: true })`.

Crisis keyword detection: client-side check immediately on input change (before sendUserMessage). Crisis keywords: `['abuse', 'suicidal', 'emergency', 'immediate danger', 'restraining order']`. `const hasCrisis = keywords.some(kw => inputText.toLowerCase().includes(kw))`.

CrisisCard key prop: `<CrisisCard key={lastUserMessageId} isVisible={hasCrisisKeyword} onDismiss={() => setDismissed(true)} />`. Changing the key prop when a new user message is sent resets the dismissed state.
Confirmation modal: shadcn/ui `AlertDialog` for mark-complete confirmation: `import { AlertDialog, AlertDialogAction, AlertDialogCancel, ... } from '@/components/ui/alert-dialog'`.
Anti-pattern: do NOT debounce the crisis keyword check — it should be synchronous on every input change for immediate CrisisCard display.

tasks.title: Ready-for-joint view (/cases/:id/ready, synthesis display, enter CTA)
tasks.summary: Implement ReadyForJointView.tsx: synthesis card, 'Enter Joint Session' CTA calling jointChat/enter.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches jointChat/mySynthesis; skeleton while loading
Synthesis card with privacy label
Synthesis rendered as markdown
'Enter Joint Session →' primary button
Calls jointChat/enter; routes to /joint on success
Handles case already JOINT_ACTIVE (other party entered)
stack_practices: Markdown rendering: use `react-markdown` package. `<ReactMarkdown className='prose prose-sm max-w-none'>{synthesisText}</ReactMarkdown>`. Install `@tailwindcss/typography` plugin for `prose` classes.
Idempotent enter: the `jointChat/enter` mutation returns successfully even if case is already `JOINT_ACTIVE`. No special client-side handling needed — `navigate('../joint')` on any success.

Skeleton: `const synthesis = useQuery(api.jointChat.mySynthesis, { caseId })`. When `synthesis === undefined`, show skeleton. When `synthesis === null`, show a loading/pending state ('Synthesis is being generated...').
Anti-pattern: do NOT render raw markdown as HTML via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. Always use `react-markdown` with the `rehype-sanitize` plugin to prevent XSS from server-generated content.

tasks.title: Joint chat view (/cases/:id/joint) with COST_LIMITED state handling
tasks.summary: Implement JointChatView.tsx: real-time reactive messages, party avatars, Coach messages, @-mention, closure banner, 'My guidance' Sheet, COST_LIMITED and read-only states.
acceptance_criteria: Reactive messages auto-update both clients
Party avatar colors correct; ⟡ glyph + coach-accent on Coach messages
isIntervention messages: warning-tint background
'Coach is thinking...' indicator for STREAMING Coach row
@-mention: sendUserMessage triggered normally (backend detects @Coach)
Closure proposal banner with Confirm/Reject buttons
'My guidance' Sheet with synthesisText
COST_LIMITED: banner + hidden Draft Coach + manual messages still work
CLOSED_*: input and Draft Coach hidden; 'case is closed' banner
stack_practices: Real-time: `const messages = useQuery(api.jointChat.messages, { caseId })`. BOTH clients share the same reactive subscription — Convex pushes updates to all subscribers when any jointMessage row changes.
Party identification: `const caseData = useOutletContext<{ case: Case; currentUserId: Id<'users'> }>()`. A message is 'mine' if `msg.authorUserId === currentUserId`.

Mobile sheet for Draft Coach: detect viewport via `useMediaQuery('(max-width: 768px)')` hook (implement with `window.matchMedia` and a `useEffect` listener, or use a CSS media query + conditional classNames).
Closure proposal banner: query `cases/partyStates` and check `other.closureProposed === true`. Render the banner above the input when true.
'My guidance' Sheet: `useQuery(api.jointChat.mySynthesis, { caseId })` — reuse the same query as ReadyForJointView. Render in a `<Sheet>` triggered by a button in the top nav area.
COST_LIMITED state: `caseData.case.status === 'COST_LIMITED'` → show banner, hide `<DraftCoachPanel>`, but keep `<MessageInput>` enabled (parties can still message each other).

Anti-pattern: do NOT have the client detect @mentions and set a special flag in `sendUserMessage` args. The mutation body handles @-mention detection. The client just calls `sendUserMessage({ caseId, content })` normally.

tasks.title: Draft Coach panel (side panel/bottom sheet, draft ready card, send gate)
tasks.summary: Implement DraftCoachPanel.tsx: 420px right panel on desktop, full-screen Sheet on mobile. Draft conversation, 'Draft it for me' button, DraftReadyCard with Send/Edit/Refine/Discard actions.
acceptance_criteria: Opens on '✨ Draft with Coach' click, calls draftCoach/startSession
Private label in panel
ChatWindow connected to draftCoach/sendMessage
'Draft it for me' posts canonical message
DraftReadyCard when finalDraft populated: Send/Edit/Refine/Discard
'Send this message': calls sendFinalDraft; panel closes; message in joint chat
'Edit before sending': transfers to main input; does NOT auto-send
Esc closes panel; focus returns to joint-chat input
Focus moves to draft textarea when panel opens
stack_practices: Panel layout: on desktop, use absolute/fixed positioning (420px right-side panel within the JointChatView layout). On mobile (<768px), use shadcn/ui `Sheet` with `side='bottom'` and `className='h-screen'`.
Focus management: `useEffect(() => { if (isOpen) textareaRef.current?.focus(); }, [isOpen])`. Use `useRef<HTMLTextAreaElement>` for the textarea reference.

Esc close + focus return: `onKeyDown` on the panel container `if (e.key === 'Escape') { closePanel(); jointChatInputRef.current?.focus(); }`. The `jointChatInputRef` is passed as a prop from JointChatView.

'Edit before sending': set the main joint chat input value via a state setter prop passed from JointChatView: `onTransferDraft(finalDraft)`. This is a controlled component pattern — the parent holds the input state.

Reactive draft session: `const session = useQuery(api.draftCoach.session, { caseId })`. The `finalDraft` field appears on the session when the AI completes readiness detection. React to `session?.finalDraft !== null` to show `DraftReadyCard`.
Anti-pattern: do NOT auto-send when the AI produces a finalDraft. The send gate is an explicit user action. The DraftReadyCard must render with Send as a button requiring an explicit click.
Panel z-index: ensure the panel appears above the joint chat message list but below modals (z-index ordering: messages < panel < Sheet/modals < Dialog).

tasks.title: Closure flow UI and closed case view (/cases/:id/closed)
tasks.summary: Implement closure modal (Resolved/Not resolved/Take a break), rejection flow, and /cases/:id/closed read-only archive view with tabs.
acceptance_criteria: Closure modal: three options with correct mutations
Resolved: summary textarea required (min 10 chars); calls proposeClosure
Not resolved: warning styling; calls unilateralClose
Take a break: closes modal, no state change
Rejection: rejectClosure; clears banner
Confirmation: confirmClosure; both parties route to /closed (reactive)
/cases/:id/closed: outcome label, closure summary, read-only tabs
Input and Draft Coach absent in closed view
stack_practices: Closure modal: shadcn/ui `Dialog` with custom content (three option buttons). Open via `useState<boolean>` triggered by the '[Close]' button in top nav.
Tab component for /closed view: shadcn/ui `Tabs`, `TabsList`, `TabsTrigger`, `TabsContent` from `@/components/ui/tabs`. Default tab: 'Joint Chat'.

Reactive route update to /closed: when `caseData.case.status` becomes any `CLOSED_*` value (detected in JointChatView's `useEffect`), call `navigate('../closed', { replace: true })`. Both parties' UIs update within ~200ms since they share the same reactive query.
Closed view read-only: render `<ChatWindow messages={messages} readOnly={true} />`. Add a `readOnly` prop to ChatWindow that hides `<MessageInput>` and the Draft Coach button.

Summary textarea validation: `const isValid = closureSummary.trim().length >= 10`. Disable the 'Propose Resolution' button when `!isValid`. Show character count: `{closureSummary.length} characters (minimum 10)`.

Anti-pattern: do NOT render the other party's private coaching tab in the closed view. The tab list is: 'Joint Chat', 'My Private Coaching', 'My Guidance'. Accessing `/cases/:id/private` from the closed view for another user's data must still return FORBIDDEN from the Convex query.

tasks.title: Solo mode UI (PartyToggle, useSoloActingParty hook, all views integrated)
tasks.summary: Implement PartyToggle and useSoloActingParty hook with URL param state. Integrate into all data queries.
acceptance_criteria: PartyToggle: segmented control; only rendered when case.isSolo=true
Toggle state in ?as=initiator|invitee URL param; preserved on refresh
useSoloActingParty(): returns role for selected party
All queries respect toggle for solo mode
Both synthesisTexts differ in solo mode
PartyToggle absent when case.isSolo=false
Solo cases show 'Solo test' badge on dashboard and PhaseHeader
stack_practices: URL search param: `const [searchParams, setSearchParams] = useSearchParams()` from React Router v6. `const actingAs = searchParams.get('as') ?? 'initiator'`. Toggle: `setSearchParams({ as: newParty })`. This preserves the value across browser refresh.

useSoloActingParty hook: `export function useSoloActingParty(isSolo: boolean): 'INITIATOR' | 'INVITEE'` — reads URL param, returns role string. Use across PrivateCoachingView, JointChatView, ReadyForJointView.

Query integration: Convex queries that filter by role need the acting party's role passed as an argument. For `privateCoaching/myMessages`, the backend already filters by `userId`. For solo mode, the backend must also accept an optional `role` filter to distinguish same-userId but different-role message sets. Update the query args accordingly.
PartyToggle component: render as two buttons styled as a segmented control. Use `coach-accent` color (CSS custom property) for the active state. Visible ONLY when `case.isSolo === true`.
Anti-pattern: do NOT store the solo party selection in `localStorage` or `useState` — use the URL param so the selection survives refresh and can be bookmarked/shared for debugging.
Badge: a small `Solo` component. Render inline in the dashboard case row and PhaseHeader when `case.isSolo`.

tasks.title: Profile page and logout
tasks.summary: Implement /profile: display name edit, sign-out.
acceptance_criteria: /profile: read-only email, editable displayName
Saving calls users/updateDisplayName; success toast
'Sign out' calls Convex Auth signOut; redirects to /
Logged-out access to /profile redirects to /login
Profile accessible from top nav
stack_practices: Sign out: `const { signOut } = useAuthActions(); await signOut()` — clears client session. Then `navigate('/')`. This is the Convex Auth hook — do NOT call any custom endpoint.
Display name mutation: `const updateDisplayName = useMutation(api.users.updateDisplayName)`. Optimistic UI: update local state immediately, revert on error.
Current user query: `const currentUser = useQuery(api.users.getCurrentUser)` — define a simple query that calls `requireAuth(ctx)` and returns the users row.
Profile route guard: use the `<RequireAuth>` wrapper from T20.
Anti-pattern: do NOT expose the users table fields that shouldn't be editable (email, role, createdAt) as form fields. Only render `displayName` as an editable input.

tasks.title: Admin template UI (list, edit with version history, audit log)
tasks.summary: Implement admin-only routes: /admin/templates, /admin/templates/:id, /admin/audit.
acceptance_criteria: Non-admins get 403 page (server-side role check)
TemplatesListPage: table with relevant columns; [+ New Template] button
Pinned Cases Count computed via query
TemplateEditPage: two-pane form + version history; 'Publish New Version' button
Version comparison modal: side-by-side text view
'Archive Template': confirmation with pinned case count warning
AuditLogPage: filterable table; row click opens Drawer with metadata JSON
Audit log read-only
stack_practices: Admin route guard: create `<RequireAdmin>` wrapper that reads `currentUser.role === 'ADMIN'`. Render a 403 page (`<Forbidden403Page />`) when not admin. The server-side check is in the Convex queries/mutations — the client-side check is for UX only.

Table component: shadcn/ui `Table`, `TableHeader`, `TableRow`, `TableHead`, `TableBody`, `TableCell` from `@/components/ui/table`. Do NOT use a data-grid library — the tables are simple enough for the primitives.

Version comparison modal: shadcn/ui `Dialog` with two side-by-side `<pre>` or `<textarea readOnly>` blocks showing globalGuidance for v(n-1) and v(n). Simple text comparison — no diff algorithm needed.

Drawer for audit log: shadcn/ui `Sheet` (not `Drawer` if not available) with `side='right'`. Show `
{JSON.stringify(metadata, null, 2)}
`.

Filterable audit log: filter by actor email using `useState<string>` and `Array.filter()` on the client. The full audit log is loaded via `admin/auditLog/list` query. If the log grows large, add server-side filtering later — for v1, client-side is fine.
Anti-pattern: do NOT bypass the server-side `assertAdmin` check by relying only on the client-side `<RequireAdmin>` guard. The Convex function must enforce authorization independently.

tasks.title: Accessibility pass (WCAG 2.1 AA, keyboard navigation, screen reader, reduced motion)
tasks.summary: Systematic accessibility audit and remediation across all 12+ routes.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly one <main> per page; <nav> and <aside> landmarks
Chat regions: role='log', aria-live='polite'
Streaming messages announce 'Coach is replying' once per response
All modals trap focus; Esc closes; focus returns to trigger
Route change moves focus to h1
Draft Coach panel: focus moves to textarea on open
prefers-reduced-motion: all animations disabled
All text ≥ 4.5:1 contrast in light and dark mode
Keyboard-only critical path test passable
All buttons have visible text or aria-label
stack_practices: axe-core audit: run `@axe-core/react` in development mode by adding `import('@axe-core/react').then(axe => axe.default(React, ReactDOM, 1000))` in `main.tsx` (dev-only). This logs accessibility violations to the browser console during development.

Focus trap: shadcn/ui Dialog and Sheet components (built on Radix UI) handle focus trapping automatically via `@radix-ui/react-focus-scope`. Do NOT implement a custom focus trap — Radix's is WCAG-compliant.

Route change focus: add a React Router `useLocation` subscription in App.tsx: `useEffect(() => { document.querySelector('h1')?.focus(); }, [location.pathname])`. The h1 must have `tabIndex={-1}` to be programmatically focusable.
aria-live for chat: add `role='log' aria-live='polite' aria-relevant='additions'` to the message list container in ChatWindow. New messages will be announced by screen readers as they arrive.

Streaming message announcement: render a STREAMING message as `<div role='status' aria-label='Coach is replying'>` (once). Do NOT add aria-label per token — that causes repeat announcements. Remove the `role='status'` when status changes to COMPLETE.

Focus ring: in `src/index.css`, add `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; }` globally. Then add `outline-none` ONLY where a custom `:focus-visible` replacement is provided.
Contrast check tool: use the `are-you-ok` CLI (`npx are-you-ok`) or browser DevTools color picker against the design tokens to verify all text/background combinations meet 4.5:1.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use `outline: none` without providing a `:focus-visible` replacement. This breaks keyboard navigation for all users, including those who don't use assistive technology.

tasks.title: Playwright E2E test suite (all 7 spec files, multi-browser)
tasks.summary: Implement the full Playwright test suite: 7 spec files, multi-browser projects, CLAUDE_MOCK=true stub.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE_MOCK=true: deterministic stub with configurable delays; no live Anthropic calls
auth.spec.ts: register, logout, login; Google OAuth mocked
solo-full-flow.spec.ts: complete end-to-end in chromium, firefox, webkit
invite-flow.spec.ts: two browser contexts; User A creates, User B accepts
draft-coach.spec.ts: verify send gate (Generate Draft does NOT send; Send does)
privacy.spec.ts: FORBIDDEN for cross-party private message access; Coach output clean
admin-templates.spec.ts: create, publish v1, create case (pinned v1), publish v2, verify pinning, archive
state-machine.spec.ts: illegal transitions, idempotency, consumed token
Three browser projects; axe-core scan in solo-full-flow
All specs pass 100% with CLAUDE_MOCK=true
stack_practices: Playwright config: `playwright.config.ts` at root. Define three projects: `{ name: 'chromium', use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'] } }`, `{ name: 'firefox', use: { ...devices['Desktop Firefox'] } }`, `{ name: 'webkit', use: { ...devices['Desktop Safari'] } }`. Set `testDir: 'e2e/'`.

CLAUDE_MOCK stub: implement as a check at the top of each AI action: `if (process.env.CLAUDE_MOCK === 'true') { return CANNED_RESPONSES[role] }`. Define canned responses in `convex/lib/mockResponses.ts`. For streaming stubs, use a loop that emits the canned text character-by-character with `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 20))` delays.

Two-context invite test: `const contextA = await browser.newContext(); const contextB = await browser.newContext()`. Each context has its own cookies/session. Create pages from each context independently.

Convex test client in privacy.spec.ts: use `@convex-dev/test-utils` (if available) or the Convex HTTP API (`/api/run_query`) to directly call Convex queries as User B for User A's data. Alternatively, use Playwright's `page.evaluate` to call `window.convex.query(...)` directly.

axe-core in Playwright: `import AxeBuilder from '@axe-core/playwright'`. Call `await new AxeBuilder({ page }).analyze()` after each major page navigation in `solo-full-flow.spec.ts`. Fail on `violations` array being non-empty.
Seeding for E2E: before E2E runs, call `npx convex run seed` to create admin user and templates. This is the same `convex/seed.ts` mutation from T11.

Anti-pattern: do NOT use `page.waitForTimeout(ms)` for timing — use `page.waitForSelector`, `page.waitForResponse`, or `expect(locator).toBeVisible()` with a timeout. Hardcoded sleeps make tests flaky.

tasks.title: Vitest unit tests for core utility modules
tasks.summary: Vitest unit tests covering privacy filter, prompt assembly, transcript compression, invite token generation, state machine transitions.
acceptance_criteria: privacyFilter: ≥5 adversarial, ≥3 benign, boundary cases (8 tokens → fail, 7 tokens → pass)
prompts.assemblePrompt: all 4 roles; assert required verbatim segments
prompts.assemblePrompt DRAFT_COACH: otherPartySynthesis does NOT appear in messages
transcriptCompression: over-budget reduces; under-budget unchanged; cache hit (1 API call for duplicate input)
inviteToken: 100 tokens unique, 32 chars, url-safe /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/
stateMachine: all 9 valid transitions; ≥8 illegal transitions throw CONFLICT; markComplete idempotent; decline-invite returns CLOSED_ABANDONED
All tests pass with `npx vitest run`; no live Convex or Anthropic calls
stack_practices: Test files: `convex/lib/__tests__/privacyFilter.test.ts`, `prompts.test.ts`, `transcriptCompression.test.ts`, `stateMachine.test.ts`. Keep test files co-located with the modules they test.

Vitest config: `vitest.config.ts` at root: `import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'; export default defineConfig({ test: { environment: 'node' } })`. These are pure Node tests — no browser environment needed.

Mock for transcriptCompression: use `vi.fn()` to create a mock Anthropic client: `const mockAnthropic = { messages: { create: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ content: [{ text: 'SUMMARY: ...' }] }) } }`. Pass as the third argument to `compressIfNeeded`.
Spy for cache verification: `const spy = vi.spyOn(mockAnthropic.messages, 'create')`. After calling `compressIfNeeded` twice with the same messages, assert `spy.mock.calls.length === 1`.
Invite token test: `Array.from({ length: 100 }, generateInviteToken)` where `generateInviteToken` calls `crypto.randomUUID().replace(/-/g, '').slice(0, 32)`. Check Set size equals 100 for uniqueness.

Anti-pattern: do NOT import `convex/_generated/server` or any Convex runtime module in Vitest tests — these modules have Convex-specific globals that don't exist in Node. Pure utility modules in `convex/lib/` must have zero Convex runtime dependencies.

ConvexError in stateMachine tests: `import { ConvexError } from 'convex/values'` is safe — `convex/values` is a pure JS package with no runtime dependencies. Use `expect(() => transition(...)).toThrow(ConvexError)`.

tasks.title: CI pipeline (GitHub Actions: lint, typecheck, unit, E2E)
tasks.summary: Configure .github/workflows/ci.yml with four jobs: lint, typecheck, unit, e2e.
acceptance_criteria: ci.yml: four jobs on push to PR branches targeting main
lint: ESLint + Prettier; fails on any warning or error
typecheck: tsc --noEmit; fails on any type error
unit: npx vitest run; all T35 tests pass
e2e: deploy to Convex dev; run seed; run Playwright with CLAUDE_MOCK=true; multi-browser
README documents local run commands
node_modules cached
T33 and T34 can run in parallel before T36 passes
stack_practices: GitHub Actions cache: use `actions/cache@v4` with `path: ~/.npm` and `key: ${{ runner.os }}-npm-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}`. Run `npm ci` (not `npm install`) for reproducible installs.

Convex deploy in CI: `npx convex deploy --cmd 'npm run build'` using the `CONVEX_DEPLOY_KEY` secret (set in GitHub repo Settings → Secrets). Deploy to a dedicated CI/test Convex project (not production).
Playwright in CI: use `npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox webkit` in the e2e job setup step. GitHub Actions runners need browser binaries installed explicitly.

Parallel jobs: lint, typecheck, and unit jobs have no dependencies between them — run them with `needs: []` (default). The e2e job depends on `needs: [unit]` to avoid wasting Convex deploy credits on a failing unit suite.
CLAUDE_MOCK env var: set `env: { CLAUDE_MOCK: 'true' }` on the e2e job. This activates the stub in Convex actions without modifying source code.
Seed step: `- run: npx convex run seed` after `npx convex deploy`. This populates the admin user and templates before Playwright runs.
Anti-pattern: do NOT run `npx convex dev` in CI — `convex dev` is for local development (starts a watcher process that never exits). Use `npx convex deploy` for one-shot CI deployment.
README commands: document exactly: `npm run lint` (ESLint + Prettier check), `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit), `npm run test:unit` (vitest run), `npm run test:e2e` (playwright test).
